company_name: Space Exploration Technologies Corp. Class A
one_liner: SPCX -7.8% on very low volume (0.58x) — the sector selloff hits the SpaceX-linked vehicle alongside the Meta cloud narrative, with no fundamental data available and negative sentiment.

price_action.vol_rank: Very low — 0.58x, well below 177M share 30-day average
price_action.intraday_story: SPCX gapped down from $170.86 (prior close) to open at $171.57, briefly tested $171.74, then collapsed to $156.16 before closing at $157.54. Despite the -7.8% move, volume was only 0.58x average — this is a news-driven gap down with low conviction sellers. The low volume on the decline is mildly constructive but the news catalyst (SpaceX competing in AI compute with Meta) is uncertain.

news_catalyst.headline: TechCrunch: 'Meta, like SpaceX, looks to turn excess AI compute into cash' — SpaceX compute-leasing plans weigh on SPCX
news_catalyst.summary: TechCrunch reported that both Meta and SpaceX are turning excess AI compute capacity into a revenue business — directly competing with AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud. For SPCX (the public vehicle tracking SpaceX), this narrative introduces competitive uncertainty: SpaceX's compute business is not the core business (rockets/Starlink) that SPCX tracks, but it creates noise around capital allocation. The core concern for SPCX holders is whether SpaceX's AI compute buildout diverts capital from rocket development and Starlink expansion.

technical.summary: SPCX has been in a downtrend since June 25 (from ~$160 high) with a recent bounce to $172 on June 30 fully reversed today. The 0.58x volume on the gap down is the only constructive element — panic sellers are absent. DCF is not feasible (SpaceX is pre-public with limited financial data). Negative sentiment score (-0.164) is the most negative in the chip/space sector.

wyckoff_narrative: SPCX lacks a clear Wyckoff pattern. The stock has been trending lower from $172 to $156 in the past week with consistently below-average volume, suggesting a controlled markdown rather than panic distribution. No clear SC or AR has formed to initiate a proper accumulation base.

valuation.summary: DCF not feasible — SpaceX's financial structure is private and not accessible for standard DCF modeling. Valuation is narrative-driven.

key_concerns: -0.164 sentiment score, most negative in watchlist AI/space names
Not a pure-play stock — tracks a private company
fundamentals.summary: Insufficient fundamental data. SPCX tracks SpaceX but the underlying company is private with limited transparency.

risks: Further below $150 and SPCX has limited technical support
Negative sentiment (-0.164) and sector selloff momentum could sustain pressure
No DCF floor to anchor fundamental buyers
